threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the 38 bus comes screeching to a halt two inches from the curb, while seasoned locals just step aside like it's nothing.
Last night on the 45, a guy tried to sell everyone homemade cocktails from a backpack. Smelled like a proper pub; passengers were torn between curiosity and disbelief.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler argue in Turkish with their mom over the last Stück Kuchen at the local bakery. Pure Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar where they serve homemade pickles alongside craft beer. It's a uniquely Berlin twist that surprises every time.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to stock up on snacks before a BART closure, while the newbies just stand confused at the Civic Center exit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting for a bus on Eddy, smooth jazz playing from his portable speaker. Only in the TL, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about the Gaudí façades, but the real charm is in the hidden squares where locals sip café amb llet, not crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewilderment when the tram doors don’t open automatically—every local knows to press the button.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is just the Underground. Real locals call the Overground the 'Coffee Line' for all the cafés along the route.
Saw a guy on the N381 loudly arguing with a lamp post last night, thought it was broken until I realized he was trying to convince it he was right.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a tango lesson in the park next to my building and everyone just joined in, like it was the most normal thing ever.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a beer at 8 am in Friedrichshain and nobody bats an eye. It's just another day at a Kiosk, living that Berliner freedom.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the Civic Center BART station after a loud bang – the tourists are all still waiting for their rides.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 this morning, and a saxophonist hopped on, turned the whole bus into a mini concert. Almost forgot we were all late to work.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for hipsters, but many families have lived here for generations, preserving hidden gardens and local bakeries.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they’re still using Google Maps to navigate the streets instead of just following the sun.
